compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry identifies P1, P2, P3, and P4. Visual evidence confirms only two containers (one yellow, one black). No biological specimens (plants) are visible in either container. The white rabbit anchor is absent.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty. Contains only soil and a sensor probe.
plant_audit.P2_P3_P4_Black_Pot: Empty. Contains only soil and scattered white debris (likely eggshell fragments).

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, and featureless. No signs of germination or active root systems.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of electronic sensor probe in yellow pot; white particulate matter (eggshells) in black pot; total absence of flora.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic debris or leaf litter detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, empty environment. No growth, no decay (as the plants were already absent), and no movement of the pots. The only change is the variation in lighting intensity between frames.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of plant life in the provided images. The environment is strictly a soil-filled container setup without active botanical occupants.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments as potential soil amendment
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to map the physical containers against the provided registry; I validated this by cross-referencing the pot colors and shapes. The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens (P1-P4). The pots contain only soil and inert materials. There is no evidence of 'Systemic Loss' occurring during the 5-day window, as the pots appear to have been empty at the start of the sequence. The environment is currently a dormant, non-planted soil system.
